items.name: NVIDIA Corporation
items.headline: NVIDIA EVP Worldwide Field Operations Puri to retire; Parker appointed successor
whatChanged: Ajay K. Puri, EVP Worldwide Field Operations and 21-year NVIDIA veteran, notified the company on June 28, 2026 of his intention to retire effective upon his successor's start date; he will remain in a senior advisory role for transition

Nicholas Parker, age 55, appointed EVP Worldwide Field Operations effective on or about August 24, 2026, joining from Microsoft where he was EVP and Chief Business Officer of Worldwide Sales & Solutions (2025–2026)

Parker's compensation package: $1M annual base salary, $5M sign-on bonus, $1.5M target annualized cash variable compensation (pro-rated for FY27), $35M target-value RSU grant vesting over ~4 years, and $5M target-value performance-based RSUs vesting on 3-year TSR vs S&P 500
items.decisionTakeaway: The planned retirement of a long-tenured sales leader and replacement by a senior Microsoft commercial executive signals a leadership transition in NVIDIA's global field operations as it scales its AI infrastructure go-to-market, with no disruption to strategy or financials indicated.

items.name: Ford Motor Company
items.headline: Ford reports Q2 2026 U.S. sales results
whatChanged: Ford filed a news release dated July 2, 2026 reporting second quarter 2026 U.S. sales results
The filing is under Items 8.01 and 9.01, incorporating the sales press release as Exhibit 99
No specific sales figures are disclosed in the 8-K body itself; the exhibit contains the detailed results
items.decisionTakeaway: Ford's Q2 2026 U.S. sales release provides a demand read for the quarter but the 8-K body contains no financial figures or guidance changes.

items.name: Lucid Group, Inc.
items.headline: Lucid Q2 deliveries rise 28% QoQ; major leadership overhaul with new CFO
whatChanged: Q2 2026: produced 4,774 vehicles (down from 5,500 in Q1) and delivered 3,953 vehicles (up from 3,093 in Q1), narrowing the production-delivery gap
CFO Taoufiq Boussaid departing after handover; Alexander De Bock (former CFO of TI Automotive) named incoming CFO
CEO Napoli halves direct reports and appoints six leaders across finance, technology, customer, transformation, digital, and program management functions
Lucid Technologies established as a distinct business unit under new President/Chief Digital Officer Kay Stepper, covering robotaxis, AI, autonomy, ADAS, and enterprise IT
Organizational changes build on prior-week measures to reduce complexity and align manufacturing capacity with demand
items.decisionTakeaway: Lucid delivered a QoQ delivery improvement and a significant leadership restructuring under CEO Napoli that replaces the CFO and consolidates accountability, but production declined and the company remains pre-profitability with Q2 financial results not yet disclosed.

items.name: Tesla, Inc.
items.headline: Tesla 8-K Announces Q2 2026 Results Press Release
whatChanged: Tesla filed an 8-K under Item 2.02 announcing publication of a press release dated July 2, 2026, reporting results of operations and financial condition
The press release is attached as Exhibit 99.1 but its content was not included in the provided filing text, so no specific financial metrics, guidance changes, or operational updates can be extracted

Prior comparable 8-K (Q1 2026, filed April 22) showed total revenue of $22.4B (+16% YoY), GAAP operating income of $0.9B (+136% YoY), and free cash flow of $1.4B — providing the baseline against which Q2 results would be assessed
The July 2 timing is consistent with a Q2 2026 earnings release, approximately 10 weeks after the Q1 report
items.decisionTakeaway: Tesla announced Q2 2026 results via 8-K, but the press release exhibit content was not provided in the filing text, preventing assessment of whether revenue, margins, or cash flow improved or deteriorated versus the Q1 baseline of $22.4B revenue and 4.2% operating margin.

items.name: Columbia Financial, Inc.
items.headline: CLBK stockholders and MHC members approve MHC conversion and Northfield acquisition
whatChanged: Stockholders approved the Plan of Conversion and Reorganization converting Columbia Bank MHC from mutual to fully public stock holding company form at the June 25, 2026 annual meeting
Stockholders simultaneously approved the acquisition of Northfield Bancorp, Inc., to close upon completion of the Conversion
MHC depositors approved the Conversion at a Special Meeting of Members held June 29, 2026
Resolicitation of maximum purchasers in the subscription offering of the new Maryland successor corporation concluded June 30, 2026
items.decisionTakeaway: All required approvals for CLBK's MHC-to-public conversion and simultaneous Northfield Bancorp acquisition are now secured and the subscription offering has concluded, clearing the path to deal closing.

items.name: Gentherm Incorporated
items.headline: Gentherm enters $550M credit facility, reveals Modine SpinCo acquisition plan
whatChanged: Gentherm entered a Third Amended and Restated Credit Agreement (June 29, 2026) providing a $550M secured five-year revolving credit facility, replacing the prior $550M facility dated June 10, 2022, with Bank of America as administrative agent

Filing discloses a proposed transaction among Gentherm, Modine Manufacturing Company, and SpinCo (Modine's Performance Technologies business); an S-4 with proxy statement/prospectus was filed July 2, 2026 for Gentherm shareholder vote

Credit agreement includes specific provisions for 'Mirror Transactions,' 'Mirror Indebtedness,' and 'TopCo Restructuring,' with Change of Control definition explicitly excluding consummation of the Mirror Transactions and TopCo Restructuring

Pricing tiers set at term SOFR/alternative currency + 1.125%–2.000% margin and commitment fee of 0.150%–0.250%, based on consolidated net leverage ratio; financial covenants require minimum interest coverage and maximum net leverage ratios

Credit agreement permits the Mirror Transactions as an Investment under Section 7.02 and includes a covenant (Section 6.17) for Mirror SpinCo Matters, indicating the facility is structured to finance the proposed Modine transaction
items.decisionTakeaway: Gentherm has secured a new $550M five-year revolver and filed an S-4 proxy/prospectus for a proposed combination with Modine's Performance Technologies spin-off, signaling an imminent transformative M&A vote requiring shareholder approval.

keyMetrics.revolvingCreditFacility: $550M secured 5-year revolver (replaces prior facility dated June 10, 2022)
keyMetrics.interestMarginRange: 1.125% to 2.000% over term SOFR/CORRA/EURIBOR/SONIA (based on consolidated net leverage ratio)
keyMetrics.commitmentFeeRange: 0.150% to 0.250% per annum (based on consolidated net leverage ratio)

items.name: Gentherm Incorporated
items.headline: Gentherm enters Third Amended Credit Agreement for pending Modine/SpinCo deal
whatChanged: Gentherm entered into a Third Amended and Restated Credit Agreement dated June 29, 2026, amending and restating the Second Amended and Restated Credit Agreement dated June 10, 2022, with Bank of America as Administrative Agent

The agreement includes extensive provisions for 'Mirror Transactions,' 'Mirror Indebtedness,' 'TopCo Restructuring,' and 'Mirror SpinCo Matters,' indicating the facility is structured to support the pending acquisition of Modine's Performance Technologies business (SpinCo)

Initial pricing set at 1.125% over Term SOFR for Term SOFR/Alternative Currency Loans and LC Fees, 0.125% for Base Rate Loans, and 0.150% commitment fee, with a five-tier pricing grid based on Consolidated Net Leverage Ratio ranging from <1.00x to >3.25x
The 'Available Amount' basket includes a floor of the greater of $56,000,000 or 30% of TTM Consolidated EBITDA, plus 50% of Consolidated Net Income, for investments and restricted payments
JPMorgan, HSBC, PNC, and Wells Fargo serve as Co-Syndication Agents; Fifth Third Bank as Documentation Agent; BofA Securities as Sole Bookrunner
items.decisionTakeaway: Gentherm's new credit agreement is being put in place to finance the pending Modine/SpinCo acquisition, with pricing and covenant terms that reveal the deal's leverage profile and financing structure.

keyMetrics.availableAmountFloor: greater of $56,000,000 or 30% of TTM Consolidated EBITDA

items.name: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc.
items.headline: Eos launches rights offering at $5.481/unit, conditional on JV agreement
whatChanged: Rights offering announced: each whole Right exercisable at $5.481 to purchase one Unit (1 share Common Stock + 0.4388 warrant at $5.481 exercise price), expiring July 21, 2026
Record date July 1, 2026; rights distribution July 2, 2026; rights expected to trade on Nasdaq under 'EOSER' from July 6, 2026
Over-subscription privilege allows fully-exercising holders to subscribe for up to 200% combined, subject to proration
Completion of the Rights Offering is conditional on execution of a JV Agreement and certain commercial framework guidelines by parties to the JV Transaction
Company retains right to terminate the Rights Offering for any reason before expiration
items.decisionTakeaway: Eos is conducting a transferable rights offering at $5.481 per unit (share + partial warrant) with proceeds contingent on closing a JV transaction, signaling both a capital raise and a strategic partnership milestone that must be met for the offering to complete.

items.name: Concentrix Corporation
items.headline: Q2 revenue up 2% but operating income falls 36% on restructuring
whatChanged: Q2 revenue $2.46B (+1.9% YoY) but operating income fell to $95.4M from $148.3M as gross margin compressed and restructuring/severance costs of $42.4M hit (vs immaterial prior year), affecting ~20,000 employees
H1 net income declined 31.6% to $76.9M ($1.20 diluted EPS vs $1.68) despite 3.6% revenue growth, driven by $60.2M in severance and $100.4M total acquisition-related/restructuring expenses
Refinanced $600M of 6.650% Senior Notes due 2026 with 6.500% Senior Notes due 2029, incurring $6.3M debt extinguishment costs; also voluntarily prepaid $100M of term loans
Securitization Facility expanded from $700M to $750M and extended to March 2028; $612.5M outstanding at quarter-end
Entered definitive agreement to sell a non-core business for ~€15M; recorded $6.9M held-for-sale loss in H1
items.decisionTakeaway: Operating margin compressed to 3.9% in Q2 from 6.1% prior year as the company incurred heavy restructuring charges across ~20,000 employees, and while the debt refinancing and voluntary prepayment reduce near-term maturities, the H1 earnings decline and margin pressure signal ongoing cost-reset execution risk.

items.name: Gentherm Incorporated
items.headline: Barclays consents to fairness opinion inclusion in Gentherm S-4 merger filing
whatChanged: Barclays Capital Inc. filed a consent letter allowing inclusion of its January 29, 2026 fairness opinion (Annex C) in Gentherm's S-4 registration statement
The S-4 relates to an unspecified Merger transaction involving Gentherm, with Barclays serving as financial advisor to Gentherm's Board
The opinion is referenced across multiple sections including Summary, Risk Factors, Background of Transactions, Reasons for the Merger, and Certain Financial Forecasts
items.decisionTakeaway: Gentherm is advancing an S-4 merger registration with Barclays' fairness opinion now formally consented for inclusion, signaling the deal process is progressing toward shareholder vote.

items.name: Dollar Tree, Inc.
items.headline: Dollar Tree replenishes $2.5B share buyback authorization
whatChanged: Board replenished share repurchase authorization to $2.5B aggregate, replacing prior $2.5B program approved July 2025
Company repurchased $500M of common stock in June 2026 via block trade with selling stockholders including Mantle Ridge LP affiliates
Approximately $700M remained under the prior authorization before this replenishment
New authorization has no expiration date and includes any remaining amounts from the prior program
items.decisionTakeaway: Dollar Tree's Board reauthorized a $2.5B buyback (~10% of market cap) after completing a $500M block-trade repurchase from Mantle Ridge LP, signaling continued aggressive capital return alongside activist-related share turnover.

keyMetrics.June2026Repurchase: $500 million (block trade with Mantle Ridge LP selling stockholders)

items.name: Arcturus Therapeutics Holdings Inc.
items.headline: Arcturus inks Thermo Fisher manufacturing deal for ARCT-032 Phase 3
whatChanged: Announced strategic collaboration with Thermo Fisher Scientific to support Phase 3 development and potential commercialization of ARCT-032, an investigational mRNA therapy for cystic fibrosis
Thermo Fisher to provide integrated Phase 3 manufacturing, clinical research (via PPD), and commercial readiness services through its Accelerator Drug Development solutions
Subject to regulatory approval of ARCT-032, Thermo Fisher receives exclusive commercial manufacturing rights under a separate commercial agreement
Phase 3 program contingent on positive Phase 2 results; no financial terms, upfront payments, or milestones disclosed in the filing
items.decisionTakeaway: The Thermo Fisher partnership de-risks ARCT-032's path from Phase 3 through potential commercial supply by securing an integrated CDMO/clinical research partner, though no deal economics were disclosed.

items.name: Alta Equipment Group Inc.
items.headline: Alta declares $0.625 quarterly preferred dividend
whatChanged: Board approved quarterly dividend of $625 per share on Series A Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock, equating to $0.625 per Depositary Share (1/1000th interest)
Payment date July 31, 2026; record date July 15, 2026
This is a recurring preferred stock dividend declaration with no change in amount from prior quarters
items.decisionTakeaway: Alta declared its routine quarterly preferred stock dividend at $0.625 per depositary share, consistent with prior quarters and with no impact on common shareholders or forward outlook.

keyMetrics.preferredDividendPerShare: $625 per Series A preferred share ($0.625 per Depositary Share, quarterly)

items.name: Lindsay Corporation
items.headline: LNN Q3 FY26 EPS $1.53 vs $1.78, revenue down 5% YoY
whatChanged: Q3 FY26 revenue $160.8M, down 5% YoY; irrigation revenue fell 7% while infrastructure grew 8%, with operating margin compressing to 11.5% from 14.0%
Diluted EPS $1.53 vs $1.78 prior year; net earnings $15.8M vs $19.5M, partially offset by lower tax rate and higher other income
Irrigation segment operating income fell 25% to $20.3M on lower volumes, higher input costs, and fixed-cost deleverage; infrastructure operating income flat at $5.4M despite revenue growth
Backlog rose to $136.1M from $117.1M at prior-year Q3, driven by MENA irrigation project; company expects ~$70M MENA project revenue in FY26
Share repurchases of $25.2M in Q3 brought FYTD buybacks to $80.7M; management plans further cost alignment actions to protect margins
items.decisionTakeaway: Q3 results show continued irrigation demand weakness and margin compression, but sequential improvement from Q2 and growing backlog from the MENA project suggest the trough may be forming while aggressive buybacks and cost actions support earnings.

items.name: S&P Global Inc.
items.headline: S&P Global completes spin-off of Mobility Global (MBGL)
whatChanged: S&P Global completed the separation of its Mobility division into independent public company Mobility Global Inc., trading on NYSE under ticker MBGL
Distribution ratio: one share of MBGL for every one share of SPGI held as of June 15, 2026 record date, effective 12:01 a.m. July 1, 2026
Fractional shares sold in open market with cash payments to stockholders for pro rata proceeds
Recast financial information (FY2025, four quarters of 2025, Q1 2026) to be released July 6, 2026 reflecting the spin-off
Financial advisors: Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, Citi, Evercore; legal advisors: Davis Polk, Baker McKenzie
items.decisionTakeaway: The completed Mobility spin-off removes a major structural overhang and shifts focus to the July 6 recast financials, which will establish the post-separation earnings baseline for both entities.

keyMetrics.distributionRatio: 1 share MBGL per 1 share SPGI held (record date June 15, 2026)

items.name: Jackson Financial Inc.
items.headline: JXN enters new $1.25B revolving credit facility maturing 2031
whatChanged: Jackson Financial entered a new $1.25B revolving credit agreement dated June 30, 2026, with Wells Fargo as administrative agent, replacing the existing February 24, 2023 facility (Bank of America as agent)
The new facility includes up to $500M in letters of credit and has a maturity date of June 30, 2031, with an option to request maturity extensions under Section 2.18
Joint Lead Arrangers and Bookrunners are Wells Fargo Securities, BofA Securities, JPMorgan Chase Bank, and TD Securities

Initial pricing set at Category 3 (BBB/Baa2): Term SOFR margin 1.375%, Base Rate margin 0.375%, commitment fee 0.150%, LC commission 1.250%, with pricing grid ranging from Category 1 (A-/A3) to Category 5 (<BB+/Ba1)
items.decisionTakeaway: JXN refinanced its revolving credit facility on similar terms with a five-year maturity to June 2031, maintaining $1.25B in committed liquidity and signaling continued strong bank access for an insurance/annuity company of this scale.

items.name: Avalyn Pharma Inc.
items.headline: Avalyn amends sublease to expand Boston office space
whatChanged: Avalyn signed a First Amendment to Sublease with CRISPR Therapeutics to expand its subleased premises at 105 West First Street, Boston by 4,934 rentable sq ft on the second floor.
Expansion term runs from the commencement date (subject to master landlord consent) through January 31, 2029, matching the existing sublease term.
Base rent for the expansion premises starts at $28,781.67/month through January 2027, rising to $29,645.12/month in year two and $30,534.47/month in year three.
Amendment adds one additional garage parking pass (total of two) and grants use of furniture in the expansion premises.
Amendment is contingent on master landlord consent within 30 days; sublandlord may rescind if consent is not received.
items.decisionTakeaway: Avalyn modestly expanded its leased footprint with a small, incremental rent obligation, a routine facilities action with no material impact on operations or cash runway.

items.name: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc.
items.headline: Eos files warrant agreement for 6.0M shares to Hudson Bay at $5.481
whatChanged: Eos filed a formal Warrant Agreement (Exhibit 4.1) with Continental Stock Transfer & Trust for 6,004,378 warrants issued to Hudson Bay Master Fund Ltd. pursuant to a Share Purchase Agreement dated June 30, 2026
Each warrant entitles holder to purchase one share of common stock at $5.4810 per share, exercisable for 10 years from June 30, 2026
Warrants are redeemable at $0.01 per warrant after 5 years if the stock price equals or exceeds $10.9620 on each of 20 trading days within a 30-day period
Cashless exercise is permitted; beneficial ownership cap electable at 4.9% or 9.8%
This formalizes warrant terms previously described in the June 30 8-K announcing the Frontier JV transaction with Cerberus and Hudson Bay
items.decisionTakeaway: The warrant agreement formalizes 6.0M warrants to Hudson Bay at $5.481 exercise as part of the previously announced Frontier JV financing, adding potential dilution but containing no new economic terms beyond the June 30 term sheet disclosure.

keyMetrics.redemptionPrice: $0.01 per warrant (after 5 years, if stock ≥ $10.9620 for 20 of 30 trading days)

items.name: Ambarella, Inc.
items.headline: Ambarella amends and restates 2021 equity incentive plan
whatChanged: Amended and Restated 2021 Equity Incentive Plan filed as Exhibit 10.1, approved via shareholder vote (Item 5.07)
Plan authorizes maximum 5,850,000 new shares plus up to 6,834,208 shares recycled from the terminated 2012 Equity Incentive Plan
Plan explicitly prohibits Exchange Programs and option repricing (Section 4.3)
Outside Director annual compensation cap set at $500,000 ($1,000,000 in initial service year)
Clawback and forfeiture provisions added covering restatements and Dodd-Frank requirements (Section 24)
items.decisionTakeaway: Shareholders approved an amended equity incentive plan adding up to ~12.7M shares of potential dilution (~29% of current shares outstanding) with no repricing rights, increasing the compensation pool available for retention but also future share-count dilution.

keyMetrics.sharesAuthorizedUnderPlan: 5,850,000 new shares plus up to 6,834,208 recycled from Prior Plan

items.name: National Beverage Corp.
items.headline: FIZZ FY2026: Revenue dips on fewer weeks, $3.25 special dividend declared
whatChanged: Net sales fell to $1,180.6M from $1,201.4M YoY, driven by one fewer selling week (52 vs 53); average selling price per case rose 5.2% but case volume declined 6.7%
Gross margin held flat at 37.0%; operating income declined to $230.1M from $235.5M; diluted EPS was $1.96 vs $1.99
Board declared a new special cash dividend of $3.25/share (~$304M) payable July 30, 2026, matching the prior special dividend paid in July 2024
Cash position increased to $349.5M from $193.8M; no debt outstanding under $150M revolving facilities
Capital expenditures fell to $25.1M from $36.3M; only 20,000 shares repurchased for $0.7M during the year
items.decisionTakeaway: Pricing power held and margins were stable, but a 6.7% volume decline alongside another large special dividend signals management is returning capital rather than reinvesting for growth.

items.name: Hims & Hers Health, Inc.
items.headline: HIMS enters JPMorgan receivables purchase facility
whatChanged: Hims & Hers subsidiaries XeCare LLC and Apostrophe Pharmacy LLC entered into a Master Receivables Purchase Agreement with JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. dated July 1, 2026
The agreement establishes an uncommitted facility for JPMorgan to purchase eligible receivables from the sellers at its sole discretion, up to a Program Limit
Purchases are structured as true sales of receivables with limited recourse to the sellers; JPMorgan assumes credit risk for account debtor non-payment due to insolvency or creditworthiness
The facility's purchase price is based on a Purchase Percentage of Net Receivables Balance minus a Purchase Discount, with interest tied to Term SOFR plus a Discount Margin
Additional HIMS subsidiaries may join as sellers subject to JPMorgan consent and joinder agreements
items.decisionTakeaway: HIMS has secured a new uncommitted receivables monetization facility with JPMorgan, providing potential access to working capital liquidity by selling eligible receivables on a true-sale basis.

items.name: Unum Group
items.headline: Unum promotes Walker to EVP, Chief Customer Operations Officer
whatChanged: Andrew Walker promoted from SVP to Executive Vice President, Chief Customer Operations Officer, effective July 1, 2026
Walker joined Unum in 2025 as SVP, Chief Customer Operations Officer; previously EVP/COO at Western Union with prior senior operations roles at USAA and Nationwide
He will continue leading Customer Operations — benefit operations, service delivery, and transformation initiatives — with no change in reporting structure or scope announced
No financial guidance, strategy, capital allocation, or other executive changes disclosed
items.decisionTakeaway: Unum elevated a recently hired operations executive one title level with unchanged responsibilities, indicating operational continuity rather than a strategic or leadership inflection point.

items.name: TIC Solutions, Inc.
items.headline: TIC Solutions annual meeting: all 11 directors elected, PwC ratified, say-on-pay frequency set at annual
whatChanged: Stockholders elected all 11 director nominees to terms expiring at the 2027 Annual Meeting
PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP ratified as independent auditor for fiscal year ending December 31, 2026
Advisory vote approved annual (1-year) frequency for future say-on-pay votes
items.decisionTakeaway: The annual meeting results confirm board continuity and auditor ratification with no contested outcomes, carrying no implications for forward outlook or intrinsic value.

items.name: LCI Industries
items.headline: Patrick Industries and LCI Industries Announce All-Stock Merger
whatChanged: Patrick Industries and LCI Industries announced a proposed all-stock merger; each LCI share converts to 1.2440 Patrick shares, with Patrick shareholders owning ~52% and LCI shareholders ~48% of the combined company
Combined equity value approximately $5.5 billion with enterprise value exceeding $7.5 billion; closing targeted for first half of 2027 subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals
Pro forma combined company: ~$8.1B revenue, ~$1B adjusted EBITDA (12.6% margin), $508M free cash flow, 2.1x net leverage
$150M annual run-rate cost synergies identified (procurement/facilities ~half, G&A the balance); accretive in year one, fully realized by year three
Andy Nemeth (Patrick CEO) to lead combined company as CEO; Todd Cleveland as Chair, Johnny Sirpilla as Vice Chair; 12-member board split evenly
items.decisionTakeaway: The all-stock merger creates an $8.1B revenue RV/marine/housing/transportation components powerhouse with $150M in identified cost synergies and accretion in year one, though integration and regulatory approval risks remain ahead of a targeted 1H 2027 close.

items.name: Apogee Enterprises, Inc.
items.headline: Apogee Completes $115M Kalwall Acquisition
whatChanged: Apogee completed its previously announced acquisition of Kalwall Companies from the Keller family, with the transaction valued at up to $115 million
Kalwall is a vertically integrated manufacturer of high-performance translucent daylighting solutions with a 71-year brand reputation and proprietary polymer and coating processes
The acquisition adds differentiated daylighting capabilities and architectural community relationships under brands including Kalwall, Structures Unlimited, and Kal-lite
Closing followed satisfaction of customary closing conditions; no new financial details, financing terms, or guidance updates were disclosed in this filing
items.decisionTakeaway: Apogee closed its previously announced up-to-$115M Kalwall acquisition, expanding its architectural daylighting portfolio, though the filing provides no new financial or guidance information beyond deal completion.

items.name: Aon plc
items.headline: Aon adds $7.5B to buyback; say-on-pay fails at AGM
whatChanged: Board approved a $7.5 billion increase to the share repurchase authorization, on top of ~$0.8B remaining as of March 31, 2026
Advisory say-on-pay resolution was NOT approved: 69.9M votes for vs 110.8M against
All 13 director nominees elected at the June 26, 2026 Annual Meeting; Ernst & Young ratified as auditor
Gregory Case's international assignment letter extended one year to June 30, 2027
Shareholders approved authorization to issue Class A shares and opt-out of pre-emption rights for 18 months
items.decisionTakeaway: Aon's $7.5B incremental buyback authorization (~10% of market cap) materially expands capital-return capacity, while the failed say-on-pay vote signals shareholder pushback on executive compensation that may require board engagement.

keyMetrics.newBuybackAuthorization: $7.5B (in addition to ~$0.8B remaining as of Mar 31, 2026)

items.name: Nasdaq, Inc.
items.headline: Nasdaq enters amended and restated credit agreement
whatChanged: Nasdaq amended and restated its December 2022 credit agreement into a new two-tranche revolving facility structure (Revolving A and Revolving B) dated June 30, 2026, with Bank of America as administrative agent
The new agreement introduces sustainability-linked pricing adjustments tied to a KPI Metric (Percent SBT Spend), with annual rate adjustments based on sustainability performance
Pricing grid set across five levels by S&P/Moody's debt rating; initial pricing at Level 3 (BBB+/Baa1): 0.100% commitment fee, 1.125% benchmark spread, 1.000% LC fee
Alternative Currency Sublimits of $330M for Revolving A Facility and $295M for Revolving B Facility are specified
Leverage Ratio covenant includes a cash carve-out of the lesser of unrestricted cash or $600M
items.decisionTakeaway: Nasdaq refinanced its revolving credit facilities into a new two-tranche structure with sustainability-linked pricing, maintaining financial flexibility without signaling any change in capital deployment strategy.

keyMetrics.initialPricingLevel: Level 3 (BBB+/Baa1): 0.100% commitment fee, 1.125% benchmark loan spread, 1.000% LC fee, 0.125% ABR spread

items.name: Six Flags Entertainment Corporation
items.headline: Six Flags COO Tim Fisher's contract amended to expire Dec 15, 2026
whatChanged: Six Flags amended COO Tim Fisher's employment agreement on June 25, 2026, setting the term to expire December 15, 2026.
Certain benefits under Fisher's agreement were extended through the December 15, 2026 expiration date to retain him through that period.
Fisher agreed he will not receive a 2026 annual equity grant or retention bonus payment under the amended agreement.
items.decisionTakeaway: The COO is being retained through a fixed transition period ending December 15, 2026, with reduced compensation, signaling an impending leadership departure amid broader executive turnover following the recent CFO appointment.

items.name: The Greenbrier Companies, Inc.
items.headline: Q3 EPS $0.60 vs $1.86 prior year, revenue down 32%
whatChanged: Q3 revenue fell to $576.5M from $842.7M YoY, driven by a 33% decline in Manufacturing segment revenue (railcar sales $431.7M vs $694.3M)
Diluted EPS dropped to $0.60 from $1.86 YoY; nine-month diluted EPS fell to $2.21 from $5.18

GBXL I issued $300M of new non-recourse asset-backed term notes (Class A at 5.13%, Class B at 5.30%) on Feb 4, 2026, with proceeds used to pay down the warehouse credit facility
Leasing senior term debt amended in May 2026 to add $125M delayed-draw facility and extend maturity from Aug 2027 to May 2032
items.decisionTakeaway: Greenbrier's Q3 results show a sharp manufacturing-driven earnings decline as railcar deliveries fall, partially offset by stable leasing income and active fleet-financing refinancing activity.

keyMetrics.Leasing senior term debt amendment: +$125.0M delayed draw, maturity extended to May 2032

items.name: National HealthCare Corporation
items.headline: NHC closes NHI facility acquisition, terminates master lease for 31 facilities
whatChanged: NHC/OP, L.P. executed a Partial Master Lease Termination Agreement with National Health Investors (NHI) effective July 1, 2026, in connection with the closing of the previously disclosed Purchase and Sale Agreement dated April 21, 2026
Master Lease terminated for 31 facilities across AL, KY, MO, SC, TN, and VA (the 'Terminated Facilities') as NHC acquires the real estate it previously leased from NHI
Master Lease remains in effect for 4 Florida Facilities (~717 SNF beds), assigned from NHI to National Health Realty, LLC as landlord

items.name: Jade Biosciences, Inc.
items.headline: Jade licenses JADE301 antibody program from Paragon
whatChanged: Entered exclusive worldwide royalty-bearing license with Paragon Therapeutics for monospecific antibodies targeting an undisclosed target (JADE301), covering all therapeutic areas
Up to $22.0M in development and regulatory milestones, including $1.5M dev-candidate fee (paid Jan 2026) and $2.5M upon first Phase 1 dosing
Sublicensing fees of up to ~$20.1M, mainly tied to commercial milestones
Low-to-mid-single-digit royalty on monospecific net sales; mid-single-digit on multispecific net sales, with 30% reduction if no valid patent in country of sale
Paragon restricted from new campaigns against the same target for at least five years

keyMetrics.royaltyTerm: later of 12th anniversary of first sale or last patent expiration

items.name: Kohl's Corporation
items.headline: Kohl's Amends $1.5B Credit Agreement, Extends Maturity Date
whatChanged: Amendment No. 2 to the January 19, 2023 Credit Agreement (previously amended May 9, 2025) dated June 30, 2026; primary stated purpose is to extend the Maturity Date

items.name: Dell Technologies Inc.

items.decisionTakeaway: Dell completed a tax-free redomestication from Delaware to Texas with 1:1 share conversion and no economic change to shareholders, but the new Texas charter introduces modest governance shifts including a non-voting Class D share class, Texas-exclusive forum, and a 3% derivative-suit ownership threshold.

items.name: Peabody Energy Corporation
items.headline: Peabody ups revolver to $400M, cuts pricing 25bps in Amendment No. 3
whatChanged: Revolving credit facility increased from $320M to $400M effective June 30, 2026
Applicable Margin reduced by 25 bps across all four Total Net Leverage Ratio pricing levels (e.g., Level I Term SOFR margin cut from 3.50% to 3.25%)
Lender group reconstituted: new lenders added, some departing lenders exited, commitments reallocated
Amendment No. 3 is the third amendment to the January 18, 2024 Credit Agreement (prior amendments Nov 2024 and June 9, 2026)
Post-closing covenants include delivery of lender's loss payable endorsement (30 days) and deposit account control agreements (120 days)
items.decisionTakeaway: Peabody expanded its revolving credit capacity by $80M and lowered borrowing spreads by 25bps, improving liquidity and reducing cost of capital as the company navigates a deteriorating earnings backdrop.

items.name: MSC Industrial Direct Co., Inc.
items.headline: MSC Industrial Q3 FY26 beats internal guidance on volume return, margin expansion
whatChanged: Q3 net sales $1,047.1M, +7.8% YoY, above the high end of prior ADS growth guidance of 5.0%-7.0%; volumes returned to growth
Operating margin expanded 170 bps YoY to 10.2% GAAP (10.6% adjusted), above the high end of prior adjusted operating margin guidance of 9.7%-10.3%
Diluted EPS $1.44 vs $1.02 prior-year quarter (+41.2%); adjusted diluted EPS $1.43 vs $1.08 (+32.4%)
Q4 FY26 guidance: ADS growth 6.5%-8.5%, adjusted operating margin 10.0%-10.8%

Full-year metrics tightened/improved: capex lowered to ~$90M from ~$100M-$110M, FCF conversion raised to ~95% from ~90%, interest/other expense lowered to ~$30M from ~$35M (includes $5.1M ERC tax benefit)
items.decisionTakeaway: MSC's Q3 results exceeded its own outlook on both top-line growth and margin expansion as volumes returned to growth, with improved full-year cash flow and capex metrics signaling better operational leverage heading into Q4.

items.name: Dynatrace, Inc.
items.headline: Dynatrace appoints two new directors, expands board to ten
whatChanged: Board appointed George Riedel (Class I) and Dan Streetman (Class II) as directors effective June 30, 2026
Board size increased from eight to ten directors
Class I term expires at 2026 annual meeting; Class II term expires at 2027 annual meeting
Compensation consistent with existing non-employee director policy; standard indemnification agreements to be entered
Press release announcing appointments furnished under Item 7.01
items.decisionTakeaway: Dynatrace expanded its board by two directors with no accompanying strategic or financial changes, a routine governance event with no direct impact on fundamentals or outlook.
